rulings.Case Title: KERRI JOHNSON VS. DTG OPERATIONS , INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-508396 WITH CASE CGC-09-495378
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 25, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT CAROLYN GILLESPIE BASS MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-508396 WITH CASE CGC-09-495378. rulings.Case Title: JORDAN ROSENBERG VS. CORNELL CORRECTIONS OF CALIFORNIA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Fo Rjudgment On The Pleadings; Motion To Deny Summary Judgment Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 25, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFF JORDAN ROSENBERG'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Motion To Deny Summary Judgment Motion. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND TO DENY SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS DENIED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT PROPERLY VERIFIED. EVEN IF IT WERE, THE PROPER WAY TO CHALLENGE AN UNVERIFIED ANSWER IS THROUGH A MOTION TO STRIKE, NOT A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. PERLMAN V. MUNICIPAL CRT (1979) 99 CAL.APP.3D 568, 575. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED THIS ISSUE BY NOT RAISING IT FOR OVER A YEAR. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: YASMEEN HAMID VS. BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 25, 2011, line 3, PLAINTIFF YASMEEN HAMID'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff¿s Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories 2 and 5 is granted. Defendant has provided no authority for its position that it does not have to produce the contact information for percipient witnesses. Plaintiff¿s Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatory 5 is granted. While Defendant has provided names of some of the supervisors but has refused to directly answer questions being asked and providing the names and contact information for the supervisors and employees that Plaintiff had direct ineractions with. This information is clearly relevant and there is no basis for Defendant withholding this information. Plaintiff¿s Motion to Compel Responses to the Request for Production of documents is granted in part. Defendant is ordered to provide further responses to RFPs 1 -3 and 13 -14. These are directly related to Plaintiff and relevant to this case. Defendant must provide a privilege log that specifically identifies each document being withheld on the ground of privilege. Wellpoint Health Networks v. Sup.Crt., (1997) 59 Cal.Ao..4th 110,130. As to RFPs 4-5 and 9-12, Defendant is ordered to provide all relevant documents in the three categories it had originally identified: (1) Blocked Accounts; (2) CDs; and (3) training and supervision of employees handing blocked accounts. Defendant must provide the above materials to Plaintiff by August 5th, 2011. Plaintiff¿s Request for Sanctions is granted in the amount of $4000. Defendant must remit this amount to Plaintiff¿s counsel by August 5, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MERYL MIKAL et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 25, 2011, line 17. DEFENDANT IAN JAFFE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Dr. Jaffee¿s Demurrer to the 3rd, 4th, 5th and 6th causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff¿s complaint is somewhat conclusory and lumps all of the defendants together. Plaintiff needs to plead with more particularity what each defendant said to constitute the misrepresentation(s). The Plaintiff must also plead reliance with more particularity. Plaintiff must also plead more facts to support its claim for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TIMBERLY HUGHES, VS. CYRYZ, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 25, 2011, line 11, PLAINTIFF TIMBERLY HUGHES, Motion To Tax Costs. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS DENIED. A JURY FEE DEPOSIT IS PROPERLY INCLUDED AS A COST OF A PREVAILING PARTY PER CCP SECTION 1033.5(a)(1). THERE IS NO AUTHORITY THAT FIRST REQUIRES A PARTY TO ATTEMPT TO OBTAIN A REFUND. THERE IS ALSO NO AUTHORITY PERMITTING PLAINTIFF TO REQUEST AN OFFSET FOR FEES. MOREOVER, GRANTING THE OFFSET WOULD MEAN THAT PLAINTIFF GETS DOUBLE RENT FOR THAT MONTH. =(302/LMG)
